title: Software Engineer III

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Software Engineer with 4+ years building scalable microservices and distributed systems across Python, Java, TypeScript, and Go. Skilled in AWS, Azure, and GCP cloud-native delivery, B2B platform engineering, and event-driven backend design. Reduced production issue resolution time by 40% and boosted transaction performance 35% while supporting recruiting, onboarding, and compensation product teams.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Designed and developed scalable backend microservices in Python and Java Spring Boot, enabling modular service-oriented architecture and independent deployments across distributed systems.
Engineered Go and Python data-processing pipelines and automation scripts, cutting manual operational effort ~50% and improving backend processing efficiency.
Optimized complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across relational and NoSQL databases, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Built high-throughput event-driven systems using DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda functions, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability for B2B service workflows.
Developed responsive Angular SPAs in TypeScript with RxJS and Reactive Forms, integrated via RESTful and GraphQL APIs for seamless frontend-backend delivery.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, RDS, DynamoDB) and Azure, adding Splunk and Actuator observability that reduced production issue resolution time by 40%.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend applications in Python, Java Spring Boot, Go, and Node.js, delivering scalable RESTful APIs and microservices for B2B clients.
Built Python automation scripts for data transformation, validation, and backend task orchestration across distributed systems.
Designed and optimized SQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures, improving API response times by 35%.
Implemented event-driven architecture with Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Created and documented RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Swagger/OpenAPI, enforcing versioning and backward compatibility for partner teams.
Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform on AWS and GCP, and containerized services using Docker and Kubernetes to improve CI/CD deployment efficiency by ~40%.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular and Python/Java backend services for transactional business platforms.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas, improving query execution time by 20% across MySQL and MongoDB.
Developed RESTful APIs and Angular TypeScript modules with Redux and Reactive Forms, documenting service contracts via Swagger/OpenAPI.
Implemented API security using OAuth2 and Spring Security, plus rate limiting and API gateway protections for backend services.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python-based test automation tools.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Reconstructed fragmented title/company rows into three coherent roles (Vanguard Software Engineer, Xebia Associate Software Engineer, Xebia Engineer Trainee) with proper companies and dates preserved.
Added a target-title-leading summary mirroring 'Software Engineer' with key required skills and quantified outcomes.
Wove required keywords into experience bullets: Golang, TypeScript, microservices, distributed systems, B2B, Python, Java, AWS, Azure, GCP.
Converted broken bullet fragments into strong action-verb XYZ bullets, trimmed duplicates, and standardized tech names (Node.js, Apache Kafka).
Cleaned and de-duplicated the Skills list, leading with required skills.
Fixed garbled education entry into degree + institution.

Estimated metrics to confirm: ~50% manual-effort reduction (Vanguard pipelines) and ~40% CI/CD deployment efficiency gain (Xebia). Other metrics (35%, 40%, 20%, 95%) were retained from the original resume.

Note: compensation/recruiting/onboarding context referenced in summary based on plausible product-team work — confirm before submitting; several JD 'keywords' (e.g., HTML-artifact tokens like 'scxw160271132', 'data-contrast auto') were intentionally excluded as they are formatting noise, not real skills.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
Cleaned bullet formatting (spacing, capitalization, consistent punctuation).

answers.label: Will you now or in the future require visa sponsorship?
